metadata.title: Government Schemes [India & States] Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: What are the two sub-schemes under the Export Promotion Mission?
questions.explanation: The Government of India is considering support measures worth about ₹25,000 crore for exporters under the Export Promotion Mission for six years (FY 2025-31). Export Promotion Mission was announced in the Union Budget 2025-26 as a flagship initiative. It aims to promote inclusive and sustainable export growth, especially supporting Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs). It is jointly driven by the Ministries of Commerce and Industry, MSME, and Finance, with the Department of Commerce as lead. The mission has two sub-schemes: Niryat Protsahan (₹10,000+ crore) and Niryat Disha (₹14,500+ crore).

questions.question: The Amrit Sarovar Mission was launched as part of which national initiative?
questions.explanation: The Government recently informed that over 68,000 ponds have been created under the Amrit Sarovar Mission. The Amrit Sarovar Mission, launched in April 2022, aims to construct or rejuvenate 75 Amrit Sarovars (ponds) in each district as part of Azadi ka Amrit Mahotsav. Each pond has a minimum area of 1 acre and a water holding capacity of about 10,000 cubic metres. Amrit Sarovars are surrounded by trees like Neem, Peepal, and Banyan. They support livelihoods through irrigation, fisheries, duckery, water chestnut cultivation, water tourism, and other activities.

questions.question: The Anbu Karangal Scheme was launched by which state government?
questions.explanation: The Anbu Karangal Scheme was launched by the Tamil Nadu government on the birth anniversary of former Chief Minister CN Annadurai. In its first phase, 6,082 children who lost both parents or cannot be cared for by a surviving parent will get ₹2,000 per month till 18 years of age. Aid covers cases of abandonment, disability, imprisonment, or life-threatening illness of a parent. The scheme ensures higher education support after schooling. Other initiatives include the Students’ Breakfast Programme, Covid-19 orphan aid, and self-defence training in government homes.

questions.question: PM Vishwakarma Scheme is launched by which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

answer.label: D
answer.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

questions.explanation: Prime Minister (PM) Vishwakarma Scheme has disbursed loans of over ₹290 crore to 32,000 artisans in Gujarat since September 2023. Loan sanctions have crossed ₹390 crore for more than 43,000 beneficiaries. Three-tier verification is completed for 2.14 lakh artisans, while 1.81 lakh have received skill training. PM Vishwakarma is a central sector scheme launched by Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. It preserves heritage crafts while giving artisans access to modern tools, credit, and markets. The scheme covers 18 traditional trades like carpentry, blacksmithing, pottery, tailoring, masonry, toy making, and basket weaving.

questions.question: “Capacity Building and Human Resource Development” scheme is implemented by which institution?

options.label: A
options.text: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)

answer.label: A
answer.text: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)

questions.explanation: Recently, the Cabinet chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi approved the Department of Scientific and Industrial Research / Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR/CSIR) Scheme “Capacity Building and Human Resource Development (CBHRD)” with an outlay of Rs.2277.397 crore for 2021-22 to 2025-26. The scheme is implemented by the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) and covers Research & Development institutions, national laboratories, Institutes of National Importance, Institutes of Eminence, and Universities. It provides opportunities for young researchers in Science, Technology, Engineering, Medical, and Mathematical Sciences (STEMM).

questions.question: Which is the nodal ministry for Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM)?
questions.explanation: The Union government plans to map all drinking water assets, including pipelines under the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), on PM Gati Shakti, a Geographic Information System (GIS)-based platform. Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM), launched on 15th August 2019, aims to provide Functional Household Tap Connections (FHTCs) with safe drinking water to all rural households by 2024. The mission follows a community approach with strong Information, Education, and Communication (IEC) components. Ministry of Jal Shakti is the nodal ministry of Jal Jeevan Mission.

questions.question: Which two agricultural initiatives were launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the Indian Agricultural Research Institute in October 2025?

options.label: A
options.text: PM Dhan Dhaanya Krishi Yojana and Mission for Aatmanirbharta in Pulses

options.label: B
options.text: PM Krishi Samriddhi Yojana and PM Kisan Urja Mission

options.label: C
options.text: PM Fasal Samriddhi Yojana and Mission Protein Bharat

options.label: D
options.text: Krishi Utthaan Abhiyan and National Agro Prosperity Mission

answer.label: A
answer.text: PM Dhan Dhaanya Krishi Yojana and Mission for Aatmanirbharta in Pulses

questions.explanation: Recently, Prime Minister Narendra Modi attended a special Krishi programme at the Indian Agricultural Research Institute, New Delhi. He launched two major schemes worth ₹35,440 crore to strengthen Indian agriculture. The PM Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (₹24,000 crore) aims to boost productivity, crop diversification, irrigation, storage, and farm credit across 100 districts. The Mission for Aatmanirbharta in Pulses (₹11,440 crore) focuses on raising pulse production, expanding cultivation by 35 lakh hectares, and ensuring procurement and value chain support.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the Labour and Employment Ministry to curb Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF) litigation?
questions.explanation: Labour and Employment Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya launched the Vishwas Scheme in New Delhi. The launch took place during the 238th meeting of the Central Board of Trustees of the Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF). The scheme aims to reduce litigation by introducing rationalised penal damages under the Employees’ Provident Fund and Miscellaneous Provisions Act. Several digital initiatives were also launched, including a re-engineered return filing module, user management module, and upgraded e-Office and SPARROW system for better APAR management. The board approved simplified and liberalised norms for partial withdrawals to improve member convenience and retirement security.

questions.question: Prime Minister Internship Scheme (PMIS) was launched by which ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

questions.explanation: Recently, Union Finance and Corporate Affairs Minister Nirmala Sitharaman interacted with interns of the Prime Minister Internship Scheme (PMIS) at Hampi, Karnataka. Prime Minister Internship Scheme (PMIS) was launched by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) as part of the Prime Minister’s five-scheme package to promote youth employment and skill development over five years. It targets youth aged 21–24 years who are not in full-time education or employment. The internship lasts 12 months and aims to benefit one crore youth in India’s top 500 companies.

questions.question: What is the name of the mission launched by government in October 2025 to promote high-yield, long-staple cotton cultivation?
questions.explanation: The Central Government has launched the ₹600 crore Kapas Kranti Mission to promote high-yield, long-staple cotton cultivation using scientific and technological methods. Farmers in Maharashtra’s Akola region have increased yields through High-Density Plantation (HDP) methods. These High-Density Plantation (HDP) techniques will now be introduced in Telangana with exposure visits for farmers. Around 24 lakh farmers in Telangana grow cotton, making it India’s top producer. The government will open 122 procurement centres after Diwali and launch the Kapas Kisan App for transparent slot booking and fair prices.
